\r\n\t\t\t\r\n\t\t\t\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0010]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tA. A 1st embodiment: \r\n\t\t\t\t\t
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tComposition of the A1. laser welding device 100: \r\n\t\t\t\t\t
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tThe laser welding method of a 1st embodiment of this indication is explained, referring to figure 1~figure 9. First, the composition of the laser welding device 100 used in the laser welding method of a 1st embodiment is explained. Drawing 1 is the figure showing the schematic structure of the laser welding device 100 used in the laser welding method of a 1st embodiment of this indication. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0011]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tThe laser welding device 100 is provided with the laser oscillator 10 and the Galvano scanner 20 as shown in Drawing 1. The laser oscillator 10 is connected with the Galvano scanner 20 by the optical fiber connector 11. The collimate lens 30 is arranged inside the Galvano scanner 20. The Galvano scanner 20 is provided with the 1st reflective mirror 21, the diffractive optics (DOE) 22, the Z lens 23, Z lens drive unit 24, the 2nd reflective mirror 25, the condenser 26, the Galvano scanner unit 27, the protection glass 28, and the Galvano scanner driver 29. The Galvano scanner 20 is equipped with the return photodetection device 50 with the ダイクロ mirror 40. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0012]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tIf a laser beam is emitted from the laser oscillator 10, a laser beam will go into the inside of the Galvano scanner 20 via the optical fiber connector 11. And a laser beam is adjusted in the parallel state by the collimate lens 30. Then, it is reflected by the ダイクロ mirror 40 and the 1st reflective mirror 21, and a laser beam results in DOE22. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0013]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tDOE22 can adjust the irradiation pattern of a laser beam. Specifically, DOE22 can be emitted as a laser beam with power density distribution shape which is different from that time of entering in the laser beam which entered. DOE22 is attached to the slide part and constituted so that slide movement is possible. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0014]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tThe laser beam adjusted by DOE22 reaches the Z lens 23. The Z lens 23 is used in order to amend a focal gap of a laser beam. By driving by Z lens drive unit 24, the Z lens 23 is constituted so that movement is possible. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0015]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tThen, it is reflected by the 2nd reflective mirror 25, and a laser beam enters into the Galvano scanner unit 27 via the condenser 26, and is emitted to the surface of the work 200 via the protection glass 28. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0016]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tIt is connected to the laser oscillator 10, Z lens drive unit 24, and the Galvano scanner unit 27, and the Galvano scanner driver 29 is constituted by the built-in program so that control of these is possible. Therefore, the output of a laser beam, an irradiation position, the diameter of condensing, etc. are controllable by the Galvano scanner driver 29. Thus, since it is controllable, as for the laser welding device 100, automatic program operation is constituted possible. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0017]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tWhen metal carries out the melting of the return photodetection device 50 at the time of laser radiation, by becoming the high temperature that the processing point I exceeds 1000℃, it is generated and detects the returning light (henceforth \"return light\") from the processing point I. As the dashed line RL shows Drawing 1, return light passes suitably the 2nd reflective mirror 25, the 1st reflective mirror 21, and ダイクロ mirror 40 grade, and enters into the return photodetection device 50. The return photodetection device 50 detects the light of the predetermined frequency bands (for example, 800 nm ~ 1000 nm etc.) beforehand selected based on the kind of laser beam, etc., and changes the strength of light into a voltage value. The return photodetection device 50 can acquire change of the voltage value in time progress as a waveform of the intensity of light. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0018]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tThe composition of the laser welding device 100 explained above is an example to the last, and the laser welding device 100 may have other composition, as long as the laser welding method concerning this embodiment described below can be enforced. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0019]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tA2. laser welding method: \r\n\t\t\t\t\t
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tNext, the laser welding method of a 1st embodiment of this indication using the above-mentioned laser welding device 100 is explained. According to a 1st embodiment, laser welding of the galvanized steel sheet of three sheets is carried out as a plurality of metal plates. Drawing 2 is a flow chart which shows each process of the laser welding method of a 1st embodiment of this indication. As shown in Drawing 2, a laser welding method comprises a superposition process (S101), a metal skin evaporation process (S102), and a junction process (S103), and each of these processes (S101, S102, S103) are carried out in order. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0020]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tDrawing 3 is a cross section for explaining the process of a laser welding method, and is the figure explaining a superposition process (S101) and a metal skin evaporation process (S102). As shown in Drawing 3, in the laser welding method of a 1st embodiment, each of these galvanized steel sheet 61, 62, and 63 is joined by laser welding, using the galvanized steel sheets 61, 62, and 63 of three sheets as a plurality of metal plates. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0021]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tIn a superposition process (S101), the galvanized steel sheets 61, 62, and 63 of three sheets are piled up. At this time, each galvanized steel sheet 61, 62, and 63 is pressed down with the fixture which is not illustrated from the upper and lower sides, is changed into an adhesion state without the crevice between the galvanized steel sheets 61 and 62 and 63 comrades, and is laid on the working table with which the laser welding device 100 is provided and which is not illustrated. In the state where it was laid on the working table, they are the top layer galvanized steel sheet 61, the middle galvanized steel sheet 62, and the bottom-of-the-heap galvanized steel sheet 63 from the top at order. Each galvanized steel sheet 61, 62, and 63 has the metal skin 65 by which plating processing was carried out with the zinc of the melting point lower than the steel plate 64 on the back-and-front surface of the steel plate 64 as a base material metal plate. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0022]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tAs shown in Drawing 3, in a metal skin evaporation process (S102), In the welding area W1 of the surface of the top layer galvanized steel sheet 61, carry out the spot irradiation of the laser beam R1 (only henceforth \"the plating removal laser beam R1\") for plating removal, the plating removal laser beam R1 is made to reach the bottom-of-the-heap galvanized steel sheet 63, and the metal skin 65 is evaporated. In Drawing 3, the portion which the plating removal laser beam R1 passes is illustrated in crosshatch. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0023]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tDrawing 4 is a mimetic diagram for explaining a metal skin evaporation process (S102), and shows the figure which looked at the welding area W1 of the surface of the top layer galvanized steel sheet 61 from the upper part. As shown in Drawing 4, the welding area W1 is circle-shaped. In a metal skin evaporation process (S102), it irradiates with the plating removal laser beam R1 two or more times (this embodiment 9 times) to the welding area W1 so that the molten pools 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 of circle shape smaller than the welding area W1 may be formed in the welding area W1 one by one. The path D1 of the one molten pools 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 is larger in general than 1/3 or less and 1/4 to the path D2 of the welding area W1. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0024]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tNext, the irradiation turn of the plating removal laser beam R1 in the welding area W1 is explained. The number as used in Drawing 4 described into the field of each molten pool 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 means irradiation turn. Here each molten pool 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 formed by each irradiation, It is made to correspond to irradiation turn to make the 1st molten pool 71, the 2nd molten pool 72, the 3rd molten pool 73, the 4th molten pool 74, the 5th molten pool 75, the 6th molten pool 76, the 7th molten pool 77, the 8th molten pool 78, and the 9th molten pool 79. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0025]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tAt this embodiment, the 1st molten pool 71 is formed by irradiating the center position of the welding area W1 with the plating removal laser beam R1 once first. Subsequently, in plane view, when the perimeter portion of the 1st molten pool 71 in the welding area W1 is divided into eight equally abbreviation with a peripheral direction ], the 2nd molten pool 72 is formed by irradiating the portion located in the upper left to the 1st molten pool 71. Next, the 3rd molten pool 73 is formed by irradiating the portion located in the lower right to the 1st molten pool 71. At this time, the 1st molten pool 71, the 2nd molten pool 72, and the 3rd molten pool 73 are estranged mutually. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0026]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tHereinafter, the 4th molten pool 74~9th molten pool 79 is similarly formed to the 1st molten pool 71 by glaring one by one with the upper right, the lower left, right above, the right, the left, and right under. In continuous irradiation, it is considered as such irradiation turn in order to keep the molten pool formed from continuing. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0027]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tWhen irradiated with the plating removal laser beam R1 by the above-mentioned irradiation turn, in continuous irradiation, the molten pools 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 formed in the welding area W1 one by one are estranged mutually. If it explains in more detail, the 1st molten pool 71 and the 2nd molten pool 72 will be estranged, and will have estranged the 2nd molten pool 72 and the 3rd molten pool 73. Hereinafter, the 3rd molten pool 73, the 4th molten pool 74 and the 4th molten pool 74, the 5th molten pool 75 and the 5th molten pool 75, the 6th molten pool 76 and the 6th molten pool 76, the 7th molten pool 77 and the 7th molten pool 77, the 8th molten pool 78 and the 8th molten pool 78, and the 9th molten pool 79 are estranged mutually in a similar manner. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0028]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tTemporarily, the field with which it is irradiated continuously is near, and if the molten pool by the next irradiation by the time the molten pool by the irradiation begun among continuous irradiation has solidified is made, an adjacent molten pool will form a big molten pool continuously by a molten state. Since a big hole will be vacant and iron will decrease if a big molten pool is formed, at the time of a junction process (S103) as final weld mentioned below, iron becomes less insufficient, a crevice is formed in the junction surface and a defect arises. Therefore, when the molten pool formed by continuous irradiation glares in the irradiation turn of estranging mutually, he is trying to avoid the defect on the above surface of junction in this embodiment. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0029]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tWhen nine irradiation is made by the above-mentioned irradiation turn, about 90% of field of the welding area W1 is covered by each molten pool 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79. As for the \"area filling factor\" which is a rate of the total area of each molten pool 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 to the welding area W1, at this time, it is preferred that it is 70% or more. It is for carrying out certain evaporation of the metal skin 65 corresponding to the welding area W1 by filling a high area filling factor. According to this embodiment, it is an area filling factor of about 90%, and can remove suitably the metal skin 65 of the welding area W1. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0030]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tThe metal skin 65 of the upper surface of the top layer galvanized steel sheet 61, the metal skin 65 between the top layer galvanized steel sheet 61 and the middle galvanized steel sheet 62, and the metal skin 65 between the middle galvanized steel sheet 62 and the bottom-of-the-heap galvanized steel sheet 63 evaporate, and are removed by the metal skin evaporation process (S102). \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0031]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tIn Drawing 3, although the plating removal laser beam R1 has penetrated the bottom-of-the-heap galvanized steel sheet 63, even the bottom-of-the-heap galvanized steel sheet 63 has just reached, and it does not need to reach to the metal skin 65 of the undersurface of the bottom-of-the-heap galvanized steel sheet 63. The laser output of the plating removal laser beam R1 is set up suitably. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0032]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tDrawing 5 is a cross section for explaining the process of a laser welding method, and is the figure explaining a junction process (S103). In a junction process (S103), the welding area W1 is irradiated with the laser beam for final weld (only henceforth \"the final weld laser beam R2\"), the melting of the base material metal plate is carried out, and each galvanized steel sheet 61, 62, and 63 is joined. The diameter C2 of condensing of the final weld laser beam R2 is adjusted more greatly than the diameter C1 of condensing of the plating removal laser beam R1. The laser output of the final weld laser beam R2 is set up suitably to the same extent as the laser output of the plating removal laser beam R1 in general. In Drawing 5, the portion which the final weld laser beam R2 passes is illustrated in crosshatch. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0033]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tDrawing 6 is a mimetic diagram for explaining a junction process (S103), and shows the figure which looked at the welding area W1 of the surface of the top layer galvanized steel sheet 61 from the upper part. As shown in Drawing 5 and Drawing 6, the diameter C2 of condensing of the final weld laser beam R2, The path D3 of the one molten pool 81 formed in / it is larger than the diameter C1 of condensing of the plating removal laser beam R1, and / as a result / a junction process (S103) as final weld ], It is larger than the path D1 of each molten pool 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 formed in a metal skin evaporation process (S102). In a junction process (S103), as Drawing 6 is shown in the arrow A, it irradiates with the final weld laser beam R2 two or more times counterclockwise so that the whole region of the welding area W1 may be covered. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0034]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tSince each galvanized steel sheet 61 and 62 corresponding to the welding area W1 and the metal skin 65 between 63 are removed in the above-mentioned metal skin evaporation process (S102) in this junction process (S103), Even if it welds with the bigger diameter C2 of condensing than the diameter C1 of condensing of the plating removal laser beam R1, the stoma called a blow hole is not formed, without emitting zinc gas. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0035]
\r\n[Effect]\r\n\t\t\t\t\t
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t(1) In the laser welding method of a 1st embodiment of the above, In the junction process (S103) after removing each galvanized steel sheet 61 and 62 and the metal skin 65 between 63 in a metal skin evaporation process (S102), The welding area W1 is irradiated with the final weld laser beam R2 with a larger diameter of condensing than the plating removal laser beam R1, and each galvanized steel sheet 61, 62, and 63 is joined. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0036]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tDrawing 7 is the galvanized steel sheets 61 and 62 and the figure explaining the state of zinc removal of 63, and the photograph which looked at the middle galvanized steel sheet 62 after the metal skin evaporation process (S102) in the above-mentioned laser welding method from the bottom is shown. Drawing 8 is the figure explaining the state of zinc removal of a galvanized steel sheet, and the photograph which looked at the bottom-of-the-heap galvanized steel sheet 63 after the metal skin evaporation process (S102) in the above-mentioned laser welding method from the upper part is shown. As shown in Drawing 7 and Drawing 8, in the part (henceforth \"the part W2 corresponding to the melting field W1\") of the field which extended the welding area W1 to the direction of radiation, it is in the state where the galvanization layer 65 was removed. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0037]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tThus, when a junction process (S103) as final weld is performed, the galvanization layer 65 in the part W2 corresponding to the melting field W1 is removed. For this reason, in a junction process (S103), the poor weld by blow off of steam of plating metal can be reduced, without requiring the complicated adjustment work of, for example, shifting intentionally the focal position of a laser beam which was performed conventionally in the upper part or a lower part from the surface of a steel plate. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0038]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tIt can carry out with the same equipment by the laser welding device 100 with a metal skin evaporation process (S102) and same junction process (S103) as final weld. For this reason, in order to remove the galvanization layer 65 a priori, as compared with the method of passing through the process by equipment different from final weld, plasma jet heating, welding on a laser torch, etc. can reduce facility cost and a process space, and can raise manufacturing efficiency, for example. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0039]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t(2) In the laser welding method of a 1st embodiment of the above, In a metal skin evaporation process (S102), in the irradiation turn that the molten pool formed continuously estranges mutually, it irradiates with the plating removal laser beam R1 two or more times to the welding area W1 so that the molten pool of a field smaller than the welding area W1 may be formed in the welding area W1 one by one. For this reason, the galvanization layer 65 can be removed, avoiding that the big stoma by blow off of zinc steam arises, and the welding quality in a junction process (S103) can be raised. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0040]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t(3) The path D1 of the one molten pools 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 is larger in general than 1/3 or less and 1/4 to the path D2 of the welding area W1. The time of Drawing 9 changing the ratio L [(path D1 of the molten pools 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79) /(path D2 of the welding area W1)] to the path of the welding area W1 of the path of the molten pool by the plating removal laser beam R1, It is the figure showing the time (cycle time) which a poor weld's rate of incidence and metal skin evaporation process (S102) take at a graph. The ratio L was taken along the horizontal axis and the poor weld's rate of incidence and welding cycle time are taken along the vertical axis. Cycle time becomes long, while a defective fraction becomes low so that the ratio L is small as shown in Drawing 9. The ratio L is 1/3 or less, and examination of these people showed that the case where it is larger than one fourth was suitable when it sees in the balance of a defective fraction and cycle time. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0041]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tIn the laser welding method of a 1st embodiment of the above, since the above-mentioned ratio L is 1/3 or less and is a larger value than one fourth, it can improve the defective fraction of junction products moderately, and does not lengthen cycle time unnecessarily and can carry out laser welding suitably. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0042]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t(4) In a 2nd embodiment of the above, about 90% and since the area filling factor of the molten pools 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 to the welding area W1 is high, it can remove the galvanization layer 65 more certainly and can improve a poor weld by extension. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0043]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tB. A 2nd embodiment: \r\n\t\t\t\t\t
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tNext, the laser welding method of a 2nd embodiment of this indication is explained with reference to Drawing 10. Drawing 10 is a mimetic diagram for explaining the metal skin evaporation process (S102) in the laser welding method of a 1st embodiment of this indication. The numerals same about the substantially same composition as a 1st embodiment of the above are attached, and explanation is omitted. In the laser welding method of a 2nd embodiment, the sizes of the molten pools 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 formed by irradiation of the plating removal laser beam R1 differ in the metal skin evaporation process (S102) to the laser welding method of a 1st embodiment. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0044]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tThe path of the 1st molten pool 71~5th molten pool 75 is the same as that of a 1st embodiment. The path D4 of the 6th molten pool 76~9th molten pool 79 is larger than the path D1 of the 1st molten pool 71~5th molten pool 75, and more greatly than one third smaller than one half to the path D2 of the welding area W1. The turn of irradiation is the same as that of a 1st embodiment. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0045]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tAccording to the laser welding method of a 2nd embodiment, the same effect as a 1st embodiment of the above can be produced. Irradiation turn can raise certainly the area filling factor to the welding area W1 by irradiation turn being larger than the path D1 of the molten pool 71~75 of the first half (1~5), and carrying out the path D4 of the molten pool 75~79 of the second half (6~9). \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0046]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tThe size of each molten pool 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 can be suitably changed like a 2nd embodiment of the above. In the molten pool where the turn of irradiation continues, it may become sufficient area filling factor to the welding area W1, and setting change is possible for the size and irradiation turn of a path suitably so that molten pools may not overlap. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0047]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tC. Other embodiments: \r\n\t\t\t\t\t
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t(C1) In the laser welding method of each above-mentioned embodiment, where the galvanized steel sheets 61, 62, and 63 of three sheets are piled up, laser welding shall be performed, but where a plurality of galvanized steel sheets of two sheets or four sheets or more are piled up, also when performing laser welding, it can apply. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0048]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t(C2) The metal plating board which is the target of laser welding may be a metal plating board which plates metal with the boiling point lower than the melting point of a steel plate, for example, aluminum, and tin on the surface of not only the galvanized steel sheets 61, 62, and 63 but a steel plate again. The material of a base material metal plate may not be limited to iron, either, for example, the alloy of iron and other elements may be sufficient. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0049]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t(C3) In the laser welding method of each above-mentioned embodiment, although the welding area W1 was made into circle shape, they may be other shape. For example, it may be a rectangle-like. Although point laser radiation which forms the welding marks of the letter of a spot shall be performed, it is good also as what performs line laser radiation which forms linear welding marks. A circle-shaped molten pool may be formed by line laser radiation. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0050]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t(C4) In the laser welding method of each above-mentioned embodiment, In a metal skin evaporation process (S102), it shall irradiate with the plating removal laser beam R1 two or more times so that the molten pools 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 of circle shape smaller than the welding area W1 may be formed one by one, but it may form by irradiation of a singular number time. The molten pools 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 may not be circle-shaped. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0051]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\t(C5) Although the molten pools 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 formed in the welding area W1 one by one assumed that it has estranged mutually in continuous irradiation in the metal skin evaporation process (S102) in the laser welding method of each above-mentioned embodiment, It does not need to estrange. Even if the one molten pools 71, 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, and 79 are small enough and a molten pool continues in continuous irradiation, it may be a grade which the poor weld by zinc steam does not produce. \r\n\t\t\t\t

\r\n\t\t\t\t

[0052]
\r\n\t\t\t\t\tThis indication is not restricted to each above-mentioned embodiment, and can be realized with various composition in the range which does not deviate from the meaning. For example, the technical feature in each embodiment corresponding to the technical feature in each form written in the column of the outline of an invention can perform substitution and combination suitably, in order to solve above-mentioned a part or all of a technical problem, or in order to attain above-mentioned a part or all of an effect. Deleting suitably is possible if not explained as a thing with the technical feature indispensable in this specification. \r\n\t\t\t\t
